name: format
summary: Selects the output format (binary, MZ, PE, COFF, ELF, ELF64, ...).

name: use16
summary: Switch to 16-bit code generation.

name: use32
summary: Switch to 32-bit code generation.

name: use64
summary: Switch to 64-bit code generation.

name: use
summary: Activates a CPU/feature setting, e.g. "use bits64" or "use avx2" (multiple settings can follow, comma-separated). Provided by the alternate/advanced "x86-2" package, not the standard x86 package (which instead uses "use16"/"use32"/"use64").

name: org
summary: Set the address counter for the following code/data.

name: entry
summary: Declare the program entry point. Not core language — provided per-format by the MZ/PE/ELF/Mach-O format packages, so available in practice wherever a format is selected.

name: section
summary: Core meaning: like "org", but also trims any preceding reserved (uninitialized) data. Overridden by the ELF/COFF/PE format packages to instead begin a named section with attributes, e.g. "section '.text' readable executable".

name: segment
summary: Begin a segment: a named MZ/PE segment in fasm1, or an ELF program-header segment with attributes like "readable"/"writeable"/"executable" in fasm2's ELF format package.

name: label
summary: Define a label at a given address: "label name [size] at expr".

name: at
summary: Introduces the base address expression after "label ... at" or "virtual at".

name: virtual
summary: Begin a block whose contents are assembled without being written to output. Close with "end virtual".

name: end virtual
summary: Closes a "virtual" block.

name: macro
summary: Define a macro. Close with "end macro" (fasm2) or a "{ }" body (fasm1).

name: end macro
summary: Closes a "macro" block (fasm2).

name: struc
summary: Defines a labeled macroinstruction (invoked as "label struc-name args" instead of a plain instruction), used to define structures. Close with "end struc" (fasm2) or a "{ }" body like "macro" (fasm1).

name: end struc
summary: Closes a "struc" block (fasm2).

name: struct
summary: Define a structure layout. Close with "ends". In fasm2/fasmg this isn't a core keyword itself — it's a friendlier macro built on "struc"/"namespace", provided by the bundled utility/struct.inc package (fasm1 conventionally provides an equivalent macro of its own).

name: ends
summary: Closes a "struct" block (see "struct").

name: if
summary: Conditional assembly. Close with "end if".

name: else
summary: Alternate branch of an "if" block.

name: end if
summary: Closes an "if" block.

name: while
summary: Loop while a condition holds. Close with "end while".

name: end while
summary: Closes a "while" block.

name: repeat
summary: Repeat a block a fixed number of times. Close with "end repeat".

name: rept
summary: Synonym for "repeat": repeats a block a fixed number of times.

name: end repeat
summary: Closes a "repeat" block.

name: irp
summary: Synonym for "iterate": repeats a block once per comma-separated value, binding each to a name.

name: irpv
summary: Iterates a block once per value held by a symbolic variable, converting it into a regular parameter for the body.

name: iterate
summary: Iterate a block over a list of value tuples (synonym "irp").

name: end iterate
summary: Closes an "iterate" block.

name: namespace
summary: Open a named scope for symbols. Close with "end namespace".

name: end namespace
summary: Closes a "namespace" block.

name: include
summary: Textually include another source file.

name: assert
summary: Fail assembly if the given condition is false.

name: display
summary: Print text to the assembler's console output.

name: err
summary: Force an assembly error at this point.

name: local
summary: Declare macro-local symbol names (unique per expansion).

name: common
summary: Marks a macro block executed once regardless of repeat count.

name: forward
summary: Marks a macro block iterated forward over repeats.

name: reverse
summary: Marks a macro block iterated in reverse over repeats.

name: equ
summary: Defines a textual constant (macro-style substitution).

name: define
summary: Defines a settable/redefinable symbolic constant.

name: redefine
summary: Redefines a symbol previously introduced with "define".

name: restore
summary: Discards the most recent redefinition of one or more symbols, bringing back their previous value (fasm2: symbols defined with "=:"; fasm1: symbols defined with "equ").

name: purge
summary: Discards the definition of a macro (or other instruction-class symbol) — the same as "restore", but for instructions rather than values. A macro can remove its own definition this way.

name: restruc
summary: Like "purge", but for labeled instructions (ones defined with "struc") instead of plain macros.

name: mvmacro
summary: Moves a macro's definition from one name to another (effectively renaming it, or temporarily disabling it to restore later); the source name is left as if "purge"d.

name: mvstruc
summary: Like "mvmacro", but for labeled instructions (ones defined with "struc").

name: align
summary: Pad with zeroes/NOPs until the address counter is aligned to N. Not core language in fasm2 — provided as a calminstruction by the format/utility packages (core in fasm1).

name: times
summary: Repeat the following instruction/data N times. Not part of the bare fasmg engine or its standard x86 package — commonly provided by a bundled compatibility include for porting fasm1 sources (core in fasm1).

name: db
summary: Declare byte(s) (1 byte) of data.

name: dw
summary: Declare word(s) (2 bytes) of data.

name: dd
summary: Declare doubleword(s) (4 bytes) of data.

name: dp
summary: Declare 6-byte data units, e.g. a far pointer (16-bit selector + 32-bit offset).

name: df
summary: Declare 6-byte data units (fasm1 synonym for "dp").

name: dq
summary: Declare quadword(s) (8 bytes) of data.

name: dt
summary: Declare ten-byte (80-bit) extended-precision data.

name: ddq
summary: Declare 16-byte data units (fasm2/fasmg only; matches the "dqword"/"xword" size).

name: dqq
summary: Declare 32-byte data units (fasm2/fasmg only; matches the "qqword"/"yword" size).

name: ddqq
summary: Declare 64-byte data units (fasm2/fasmg only; matches the "dqqword"/"zword" size).

name: du
summary: Declare word(s) (2 bytes) of data from a string, zero-extending each byte (fasm1 only).

name: rb
summary: Reserve N uninitialized bytes.

name: rw
summary: Reserve N uninitialized words.

name: rd
summary: Reserve N uninitialized doublewords.

name: rp
summary: Reserve N uninitialized 6-byte units.

name: rf
summary: Reserve N uninitialized 6-byte units (fasm1 synonym for "rp").

name: rq
summary: Reserve N uninitialized quadwords.

name: rt
summary: Reserve N uninitialized 10-byte units.

name: rdq
summary: Reserve N uninitialized 16-byte units (fasm2/fasmg only).

name: rqq
summary: Reserve N uninitialized 32-byte units (fasm2/fasmg only).

name: rdqq
summary: Reserve N uninitialized 64-byte units (fasm2/fasmg only).

name: file
summary: Include the raw bytes of an external file as data.

name: load
summary: Two distinct meanings. As a directive (fasm2/fasmg): defines a variable by reading a string of already-generated bytes back out of an output area, e.g. "load b:byte from 101h" or, via an area label, "load a:byte from hex_digits:10" (can even read data generated later in the same area). As a CALM command (inside a "calminstruction" block): the same idea but with a different syntax, always three comma-separated arguments (target variable, raw byte offset, byte count) operating on raw offsets rather than addresses.

name: store
summary: Two distinct meanings, the write-side counterpart to "load" in both. As a directive (fasm2/fasmg): overwrites already-generated bytes in an output area, e.g. "store value:1 at 101h" — can't touch data that hasn't been generated yet, and marks the area as "variable" (no longer readable in advance by "load"). As a CALM command: same idea with the CALM three-argument raw-offset syntax (offset, byte count, value).

name: calminstruction
summary: Defines a new instruction using the CALM low-level code-emission language. Close with "end calminstruction".

name: end calminstruction
summary: Closes a "calminstruction" block.

name: postpone
summary: Defers assembly of a block until after all following source text is processed. Close with "end postpone". Multiple postponed blocks assemble in reverse declaration order.

name: end postpone
summary: Closes a "postpone" block.

name: match
summary: Pattern-matches macro arguments.

name: assemble
summary: Passes the text held by a symbolic variable directly to the assembler, unpreprocessed.

name: arrange
summary: The inverse of "match" — builds up a text value from a pattern and stores it in a symbolic variable.

name: compute
summary: Evaluates an expression and assigns the numeric result to a variable.

name: check
summary: Analogous to "if" — evaluates a condition and sets the result flag tested by "jyes"/"jno".

name: emit
summary: Synonym "dbx": generates data of a given size directly into the output being assembled.

name: element
summary: Defines a special symbol representing one term of an expression; used with the "element"/"scale"/"metadataof" operators.

name: jump
summary: Unconditionally jumps to a label within the CALM instruction being defined.

name: jyes
summary: Jumps to a label only if the previous result-flag-setting command ("match", "check", "take", "taketext", or "transform") succeeded.

name: jno
summary: Jumps to a label only if the previous result-flag-setting command ("match", "check", "take", "taketext", or "transform") did not succeed.

name: exit
summary: Immediately terminates processing of the CALM instruction being defined (takes no arguments).

name: publish
summary: Assigns a value to a symbol whose name is given by the text held in a variable — the way to define a symbol whose name was computed (e.g. with "arrange") or passed in as an argument, rather than written literally.

name: transform
summary: Replaces identifiers of symbolic variables/constants within a text with their current values — the same substitution the "equ" directive performs. Sets the result flag to whether any replacement actually happened.

name: stringify
summary: Converts a variable's text into a string value, in place (like the "`" preprocessing operator, but producing a real string rather than a quoted-string token).

name: take
summary: Pops the topmost value off a symbol's value stack onto another symbol (destination may be omitted to just discard it, like "restore"). Sets the result flag to whether a value was actually available — using the same symbol as source and destination lets you peek without removing it.

name: taketext
summary: Like "take", but only succeeds when the popped value is symbolic (tokenized text) rather than numeric — a common way to check a value is safe to inspect with "match".

name: call
summary: Directly executes another CALM instruction by name, passing values (not preprocessed text) as its arguments — the only way to invoke one CALM instruction from another.

name: initsym
summary: Assigns text to a local symbolic variable at the time the CALM instruction is *defined*, not each time it's called (unlike a normal argument) — commonly used to let a custom CALM command reach into the caller's local scope.
